title: Public Complaints
description: Community members, parents, and students who have concerns or complaints about the district have a clear process to follow for raising those issues and getting a response. The policy outlines the steps for submitting a complaint, who reviews it, and how decisions are made at each level. Having a defined process helps ensure that concerns are heard fairly and consistently.

timeline_local.summary: This redline document shows revisions to Conroe ISD's GF (Local) policy on Public Complaints. The primary change throughout the policy is the replacement of the term 'complaint' with 'grievance' (and 'Complaints' with 'Grievances') consistently throughout the document. Additionally, the opening definitional sentence stating that 'complaint' and 'grievance' shall have the same meaning is being removed, which aligns with the terminology standardization. A minor addition was made to the informal process section clarifying that deadlines shall not be extended 'except by mutual written consent and as stated in this policy.'
changes_added: Grievances (replacing 'Complaints' in section headers and body text throughout)
grievance (replacing 'complaint' in body text throughout)
'and as stated in this policy' added to informal process deadline language
grievance (replacing 'complaint' throughout all sections)
starting at the level at which the grievance was dismissed (in Untimely Filings section regarding appeal of dismissal)

Recusal section (entirely new): 'A District employee or Board member involved in reviewing a grievance must recuse himself or herself from the review process if that employee or Board member is the subject of the grievance. If a Board member files a grievance, he or she shall be prohibited from voting on matters related to that grievance. If the designated administrator at any level of the review process is required to recuse himself or herself, the grievance shall be subject to a higher level of review.'
Grievance Record section (entirely new): 'At each level of the grievance review process, a record shall be created and retained by the District. This record shall include:'

Human sexuality curriculum parental consent requirements: plain-language summary, identification of outside speakers, dates/class periods, statement that participation requires written parental consent under S.B. 12, 30-day advance notice requirement, right to review instructional materials, right to withhold consent without student penalty, right to revoke consent at any time, consent checkboxes/signature lines, and administrator contact information.

DEI definition per S.B. 12 (89th Texas Legislature): influencing hiring/employment with respect to race, sex, color, or ethnicity except as required by law; promoting differential treatment on basis of race, color, or ethnicity; requiring training presenting race or sex stereotyping; developing policies referencing race, color, ethnicity, gender identity, or sexual orientation (with exceptions for HBCUs and federal/state law compliance); developing equity audits based on race, color, ethnicity, gender identity, or sexual orientation.
Prohibition on compelling any person to provide a DEI statement or giving preferential consideration based on provision of a DEI statement.
Prohibition on assigning DEI duties to any person; prohibition on district employees, contractors, or volunteers engaging in DEI duties at, for, or on behalf of the district.
Carve-out: nothing prohibits contracting with historically underutilized businesses or businesses owned by minority group members or women under applicable state law.

SEL provisions: traditional SEL consistent with district goals (self-awareness, individualism, self-reliance, self-motivation, communication, conflict resolution, interpersonal skills); prohibition on teaching divisive SEL concepts; affirmative list of approved ideologies including individualism, rejection of victimhood mentality, conflict resolution, entrepreneurship, financial self-sufficiency, importance of nuclear family, liberty/hard work/perseverance, and virtues of self-discipline/forgiveness/patience/kindness/determination/hope/thankfulness/reliability/honesty/industry/responsibility.

Definition of 'Gender Identity' as any belief/theory/ideology that: espouses gender as a social construct; espouses non-binary gender; espouses that biological sex should be changed to match a different gender; supports hormone therapy or medical procedures to alter a person's body to match a different gender.
Definition of 'sexual orientation' as sexual attraction including heterosexual, homosexual, bisexual, or other orientations.
Prohibition on Subject Persons providing instruction, guidance, activities, or programming regarding sexual orientation or gender identity to students in PreK–12.
Prohibition on Subject Persons using or permitting use of district resources to adopt, support, or promote Gender Identity or sexual orientation as defined in the policy.

Carve-outs: does not limit student First Amendment speech; does not limit authorized physical/mental health service providers subject to parental consent; does not prohibit single-sex organizations without political/social agenda from meeting on district property.
Definition of 'social transitioning' per S.B. 12: transition from biological sex at birth to opposite biological sex through different name, pronouns, or other expressions denying biological sex.
Prohibition on Subject Persons assisting students with social transitioning, including providing information about social transitioning.
Prohibition on use of pronoun identifiers inconsistent with biological sex as listed on official birth certificate or government-issued record (for both students and Subject Persons).
Prohibition on use of the term 'Latinx'; required use of 'Hispanic,' 'Hispanics,' 'Latino,' 'Latinos,' 'Latina,' or 'Latinas.'
Prohibition on use of 'minor-attracted person'; required use of 'pedophile'; instructional materials on pedophilia must refer to it only as an abhorrent crime.

Parent or employee right to report suspected social transitioning violations to the board of trustees; board shall oversee superintendent investigation; board shall report confirmed employee violations to Texas Commissioner of Education.
Referral of other concerns to complaint policies DGBA(Local), FNG(Local), and GF(Local).

Discipline provision: knowing violations are cause for discipline including termination of employees and contractors; non-employee Subject Persons may be immediately terminated or removed by superintendent or designee.
Requirement to distribute copy of this policy to all district employees, parents, volunteers, contractors, and third parties in direct contact with students.
changes_removed: In this policy, the terms 'complaint' and 'grievance' shall have the same meaning. (entire opening Complaints definitional sentence removed)
Complaints (replaced by 'Grievances' in section headers and body text)

After the Level One conference, no new documents may be submitted by the individual unless the individual did not know the documents existed before the Level One conference. (removed from Complaint/Grievance And Appeal Forms section)

timeline_local.notes: This is page 1 of 5 of the GF(Local) policy redline. The document is labeled 'Policy Update Page Number 45' and page 224 of the meeting packet. The primary substantive change is consistent replacement of 'complaint/Complaints' with 'grievance/Grievances' throughout the policy, and removal of the equivalency definition sentence at the top. Date Issued and Update fields are blank, indicating this is a proposed update.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's Board Policy GF (Local) – Public Complaints comprehensively replaces the term "complaint" with "grievance" throughout, restructures and expands procedural requirements at all three levels (including new timelines, recusal rules, grievance records, remand provisions, and a Report to TEA requirement), and adds new sections on Recusal, Grievance Record, Remand, Decisions Made on the Merits, Report to TEA, Availability of Policy, and Appeal to Commissioner.
changes_added: Grievances (replacing 'Complaints' in section heading 'Other Complaint Grievance Processes')
grievance (replacing 'complaint' throughout the policy text)
and as stated in this policy (added to informal resolution deadline exception)
starting at the level at which the grievance was dismissed (added to untimely filing appeal language)

Recusal [new section]: A District employee or Board member involved in reviewing a grievance must recuse himself or herself from the review process if that employee or Board member is the subject of the grievance. If a Board member files a grievance, he or she shall be prohibited from voting on matters related to that grievance. If the designated administrator at any level of the review process is required to recuse himself or herself, the grievance shall be subject to a higher level of review.

Grievance Record [new section]: At each level of the grievance review process, a record shall be created and retained by the District. This record shall include: 1. Documents submitted by the person who filed the grievance or determined relevant by school district personnel; and 2. A written record of the decision, including an explanation of the basis for the decision and an indication of each document that supports the decision. The person who filed the grievance may supplement the record with additional documents or add additional claims.
Remand [new section]: At any time, a grievance may be remanded to a lower level of review to develop a record, including when a grievance is pending before the Board of Trustees.

Decisions Made on the Merits [new section]: At each level in the grievance review process, the District shall issue a decision on the merits of the concern raised in the grievance, notwithstanding procedural errors or the type of relief requested.
Level One filing deadline changed from 15 to 60 calendar days

or had reason to know of the facts giving rise to the grievance, or the later of: a. 90 calendar days of the date the student or parent (or person standing in parental relation to a student) first knew or had reason to know of the facts giving rise to the grievance if the grievant engaged in informal attempts to resolve the grievance, or b. 30 calendar days from the date on which the District provided information to the grievant regarding how to file the grievance; and

With the principal's or principal's designee's office at the school campus at which the facts giving rise to the grievance occurred, or, for a grievance that arises from a subject matter unrelated to a campus, with an administrator at the District's central office. (replacing 'District's legal office')

If the only administrator who has authority to remedy the alleged problem is the Superintendent or designee or the grievance arises from subject matter unrelated to a campus, the grievance may begin at Level Two following the procedure, including deadlines, for filing the grievance form at Level One.
appropriate administrator as stated above (replacing 'District's legal office' in forwarding language)
principal, designee, or other appropriate administrator (replacing 'Level One hearing officer' and 'hearing officer' throughout Level One)
10 calendar days after the date the grievance was initially filed (replacing 'receipt of the written complaint')
20 calendar days (replacing 'ten' days for Level One written response)

The written response shall set forth the basis of the decision, any relief or redress to be provided and information regarding filing an appeal, including the timeline for filing such appeal under this policy and under Texas Education Code § 7.057, as applicable. (added to Level One response requirements)
20 calendar days (replacing 'ten' days for Level Two appeal notice filing deadline)
The designated Level Two administrator shall be the Superintendent or Superintendent's designee at the District's central office. (new language added at Level Two)
20 calendar days (replacing 'ten' for Level Two appeal notice deadline)
principal, designee, or other appropriate administrator who considered the grievance at Level One (replacing 'Level One' administrator in record forwarding)
hearing officer and legal office (removed, replaced by 'administrator')

The written response shall set forth the basis of the decision, any relief or redress to be provided and information regarding filing an appeal, including the timeline for filing such appeal under this policy and under Texas Education Code § 7.057, as applicable. (added to Level Two response requirements)
Level Two administrator (replacing 'hearing officer' throughout Level Two)
20 calendar days (replacing 'ten' for Level Three appeal notice deadline at both instances)
Superintendent or designee (replacing 'legal office' in Level Three notification)

The Board may delegate the authority to hear and decide a grievance to a committee of at least three members composed only of members of the Board. For purposes of an appeal to the Commissioner of Education under Texas Education Code § 7.057, a decision by this committee constitutes a decision of the Board. (new language added at Level Three)
Superintendent or designee (replacing 'legal office' in providing Board the Level Two record)

The Board or its committee must hold a meeting to discuss the grievance not later than the 60th day after the date on which the previous decision on the grievance was made. (new language at Level Three)
Board of Trustees (replacing 'administration' in Level Three appeal evidence language)
Superintendent or designee (replacing 'administration' in notice of new evidence)
a description (replacing 'notice of the nature')
or other information the Board intends to rely on (added to description of evidence notice)
five (replacing 'three') days before the hearing

The grievant may request that the hearing or meeting at which the grievance will be discussed by open or closed, unless otherwise required by law. If the grievant does not make this request, tThe District Board of Trustees shall determine whether the grievance will be presented in open or closed meeting in accordance with the Texas Open Meetings Act and other applicable law. (restructured and expanded)
not later than the 30th day after the date on which the Board or its committee considers the grievance (replacing 'at any time up to and including the next regularly scheduled Board meeting')
4. A summary of all previous oral testimony. (new item added to Level Two record list)

Report to TEA [new section]: The District shall annually submit to TEA a report on grievances filed in the District during the preceding year. For each grievance included on the report, the District shall include the resolution of the grievance and any corrective action taken.

Availability of Policy [new section]: The following shall be made available in a prominent location on the District's website and included in the Student Handbook: This policy; The grievance and appeal forms referenced in this policy; The method by which a grievance may be filed electronically.

Appeal To Commissioner [new section]: Except as may be provided to the contrary by state or federal law or other District policies, Level Three decisions by the Board may be appealed to the Commissioner of Education as outlined in Education Code 26A.

description_local: This policy outlines the process that community members and parents can follow when they have a complaint or concern about the school district that isn't covered by another specific policy. It applies to any member of the public who wants to formally raise an issue with Conroe ISD. Understanding this policy matters because it explains the steps you need to take and the timeline you must follow to make sure your complaint is heard and addressed by the district.
